prompt: Write a Python script using MDAnalysis and matplotlib that analyses publicly available molecular dynamics trajectories of botulinum neurotoxin type A light chain (BoNT/A-LC, PDB 3BON) to characterise active-site flexibility for structure-based inhibitor design. The script should: (1) download published BoNT/A-LC MD trajectories from the RCSB/Zenodo repository (cite specific DOI if available), (2) compute per-residue RMSF for the 250-residue light chain, (3) identify conformationally flexible loops near the zinc-binding active site, (4) cluster trajectory frames by active-site loop geometry (K-means on backbone dihedrals), (5) output: RMSF visualisation, cluster representatives as PDB files, and a conformational landscape plot (RMSD vs. active-site loop RMSD). Note: only published trajectory data are used; no new simulations are required.
source_citation: Derived from RCSB PDB (3BON) and published MD trajectory data; target: BoNT/A light chain domain (processed from P0DPI0); organism: Clostridium botulinum type A; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: zinc metalloprotease (select agent toxin catalytic domain)
